workflowHypotheses.title: Inefficient Project Submission and Judging Process
workflowHypotheses.problemStatement: Motion Design Awards handles hundreds of entries annually, across 38 categories, which suggests a complex project submission and judging process. The public-facing contact form and request flows on their website indicate a potential for manual intake, qualification, and routing of submissions, leading to delays and increased administrative overhead. This is exacerbated by the need for a "rigorous judging process involving an elite panel and public voting."

workflowHypotheses.title: Manual handling of support requests and troubleshooting
workflowHypotheses.problemStatement: The contact page mentions 'Technical support Need help with adding your work, payment process, or troubleshooting? Contact Support' indicating that there are likely manual processes involved in handling support requests. Without automation, addressing technical issues, especially related to submissions and payment, could be time-consuming and resource intensive.

workflowHypotheses.title: Bottlenecks in Coordinating with Judges and Managing Public Voting
workflowHypotheses.problemStatement: MDA's award process involves "a rigorous judging process involving an elite panel and public voting." Coordinating with the judges, managing their assignments, collecting scores and feedback, and integrating public votes can be a complex undertaking with potential for delays and errors if not efficiently automated.

workflowHypotheses.title: Inefficient Customer Support Request Handling
workflowHypotheses.problemStatement: Mujjo appears to struggle with consistent customer support response times, potentially leading to lost sales or customer dissatisfaction. workflowHypotheses.title: Bottlenecks in Product Development and Launch
workflowHypotheses.problemStatement: The "careers" page suggests potential workflow challenges in planning, operations, and reporting which are commonly associated with bottlenecks in product development, especially since they are a consumer goods company that needs to constantly adapt to phone model releases. workflowHypotheses.problemStatement: The reliance on Shopify for e-commerce suggests potential issues in synchronizing inventory levels with demand, especially considering the focus on handcrafted and limited-edition items. workflowHypotheses.title: Project Delays Due to Manual Scheduling and Coordination
workflowHypotheses.problemStatement: STB-kozijnen likely experiences project delays due to inefficient scheduling and coordination of montagewerkzaamheden. Reviews indicate the need for "Snellere doorlooptijd", and the company is actively hiring a "Planner kozijnenmontage", whose responsibilities include "Het plannen en coördineren van montagewerkzaamheden Afstemmen van planningen met monteurs, project". This suggests a potential bottleneck in the scheduling process, leading to slower project completion times.

workflowHypotheses.title: Quality Control Issues Due to Handoff Problems
workflowHypotheses.problemStatement: STB-kozijnen struggles with maintaining consistent quality due to problems in handoff or communication during the installation process. One review states "Slecht afgewerkte installatie waar we zelf de randen nog bij mochten kitten" suggesting a lack of quality control before project completion. They are also looking for a "werkvoorbereider" which suggests they need help organizing a project from start to finish.

workflowHypotheses.title: Inefficient Lead Intake and Qualification
workflowHypotheses.problemStatement: STB-kozijnen potentially suffers from inefficient lead intake, qualification, and routing processes. The public-facing contact and request flows "indicate manual intake, qualification, and routing risk.", requiring manual processing and potentially leading to delays and lost opportunities.
